userStories.title: Add pull-tool logger emits for curator ingestion
acceptanceCriteria: handleQueryNeighbor emits logger.info("pull-tool", "invoked", ...) with tool="query_neighbor" and resultCount matching the number of neighbor chunks found
handleQueryFeatureContext emits logger.info("pull-tool", "invoked", ...) with tool="query_feature_context" and keyword matching the input filter
When a pull-tool call returns zero results, the emit includes resultCount=0 and resultBytes=0
When handleQueryNeighbor truncates content, the emit includes truncated=true
suggestedCriteria: handleQueryFeatureContext emit includes storyId matching the passed story.id
Emit fires exactly once per pull-tool invocation, even when the handler returns early or throws

userStories.title: Consolidate acceptance verdict logger emit
userStories.description:
**Goal** — Add a single canonical `logger.info("acceptance", "verdict", { storyId, passed, failedACs, retries, packageDir, durationMs })` call at the per-story termination point of the acceptance stage.

**Motivation** — The curator's acceptance-verdict observation kind needs a single authoritative emit per story regardless of pass/fail. Currently the acceptance stage has scattered emits that are progress-oriented — no single event captures the final verdict with all relevant fields.

**Approach** — Existing emits (at approximate lines ~228, ~235, ~243 in `src/pipeline/stages/acceptance.ts`) stay as progress messages. Add one canonical verdict event at per-story termination — after all ACs have been evaluated, before the stage returns `{ action: "continue" }` or `{ action: "fail" }`. The emit fires once per story regardless of pass/fail outcome. The `failedACs` field is `[]` when passed; `retries` captures hardening pass count.

**Scope** — In: `src/pipeline/stages/acceptance.ts`. Out: any change to acceptance test execution logic, hardening pass behavior, or failure parsing.

**Interface**
```typescript
// Once per story at acceptance stage exit
logger.info("acceptance", "verdict", {
  storyId: ctx.story.id,
  packageDir: ctx.packageDir,
  passed: allACsPassed,
  failedACs: failedACs,    // [] if passed
  retries: hardeningRetries,
  durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,
});
```
acceptanceCriteria: The acceptance stage emits logger.info("acceptance", "verdict", ...) exactly once per story after all ACs are evaluated
When all ACs pass, the emit includes passed=true and failedACs=[]
When any AC fails, the emit includes passed=false and failedACs containing the failing AC identifiers
The emit includes retries reflecting the hardening pass count and durationMs as a positive integer
suggestedCriteria: The verdict emit fires on both the pass path (action: "continue") and fail path (action: "fail")
failedACs field matches the exact set returned by parseTestFailures() when failures exist

userStories.title: Add ReviewDecisionEvent bus channel for review audit routing
acceptanceCriteria: emitReviewDecision() fires onReviewDecision listeners with a ReviewDecisionEvent containing kind="review-decision"
onReviewDecision() returns an unsubscribe function that removes the listener from future emits
attachReviewAuditSubscriber() subscribes to review-decision events and forwards them to the auditor via recordDispatch()
When a review call site emits through the bus instead of calling auditor.recordDecision() directly, the auditor still receives the decision record
suggestedCriteria: Multiple listeners can subscribe to review-decision events and all receive the same event
A listener error does not prevent other listeners from receiving the event
The existing session-turn listener in review-audit middleware continues to work unchanged alongside the new review-decision listener

userStories.title: Curator plugin scaffold with observation projection
acceptanceCriteria: Curator config supports enabled, rollupPath, and thresholds with schema/default/type coverage, and PostRunContext exposes config, outputDir, globalDir, projectKey, curatorRollupPath, and logFilePath? from the active runtime/run.
The built-in nax-curator plugin is registered by default and appears in pluginRegistry.getPostRunActions(), while config.disabledPlugins can disable it.
collectObservations() returns schemaVersion=1 observations with runId, featureId, storyId, stage, ts, kind, and a plain-object payload for every emitted row.
collectObservations() reads outputDir artifacts (metrics.json and review-audit/<feature>/*.json) and projects story verdict/context metrics plus review findings when those sources exist.
collectObservations() reads workdir context manifests and projects chunk-included, chunk-excluded, and provider-empty observations, including stale exclusion reasons when present.

collectObservations() reads the active run JSONL when logFilePath is available and projects rectify-cycle, escalation, pull-call, acceptance-verdict, and fix-cycle observations from matching structured logger lines.
resolveCuratorOutputs() resolves observationsPath and proposalsPath under context.outputDir/runs/<runId>/ and uses context.curatorRollupPath as the rollup file path.

curatorPlugin.shouldRun() returns false when curator.enabled is explicitly false or no stories completed, returns true when enabled with completed stories, and warns-but-continues when review.audit.enabled is false.
curatorPlugin.execute() writes observations.jsonl to <outputDir>/runs/<runId>/observations.jsonl with one JSON line per observation.
Missing source directories, malformed source files, and per-source read errors are logged as warnings and do not cause collectObservations() or curatorPlugin.execute() to throw.
suggestedCriteria: Each observation's ts field is a valid ISO 8601 string
Observation payload is always a plain object (never undefined or null), even for kinds with no extra fields
collectObservations() handles missing source directories gracefully (no JSON files found = empty array for that source, not an error)
collectObservations() logs a warning and skips malformed JSON source files instead of failing the post-run action
The curator plugin appears in pluginRegistry.getPostRunActions() after registration
config.curator.thresholds defaults are materialized by NaxConfigSchema.parse({}) / DEFAULT_CONFIG
Relative curator.rollupPath is rejected consistently with src/runtime/paths.ts

userStories.title: Curator heuristics, proposal generation, and cross-run rollup
acceptanceCriteria: runHeuristics() implements all six v0 heuristics H1-H6 with configurable thresholds, correct severity/action targets, and expected proposal IDs for representative positive cases.
H1/H5 rules proposals stage to .nax/rules/curator-suggestions.md in v0; feature-context proposals target .nax/features/<id>/context.md and H6 remains advisory.
runHeuristics() returns an empty array for empty or non-triggering observations and uses default thresholds when config values are absent.

renderProposals() produces markdown with generated timestamp, observation count, checkbox sections grouped by target action/canonical file, and proposal lines containing severity, heuristic ID, counts, and story evidence.
renderProposals() still produces useful markdown when zero proposals fire, including observation count and a zero-heuristics message.
appendToRollup() creates the rollup parent directory, appends one JSON line per observation, and logs/returns without throwing on write errors.
curatorPlugin.execute() wires collect → heuristics → render → rollup, writes observations.jsonl and curator-proposals.md, and never lets curator failures change the post-run exit behavior.
suggestedCriteria: runHeuristics() returns an empty array when observations array is empty
runHeuristics() uses configurable thresholds from config.curator.thresholds, falling back to defaults when not configured
renderProposals() includes a generated timestamp in ISO 8601 format
Multiple proposals for the same target file are grouped under the same markdown section heading
Rollup append works correctly with POSIX concurrent writes (each observation under PIPE_BUF size)

userStories.title: nax curator CLI subcommands
acceptanceCriteria: All curator commands resolve the project with resolveProject(), load config, and use runtime path helpers to locate generated artifacts under outputDir and the configured rollup path.
curatorStatus() supports latest-run and --run <runId> modes, prints observation counts by kind, and prints proposal markdown when present.

curatorCommit() parses checked [x] proposal lines, applies drops before adds, writes accepted changes to canonical target files, detects conflicts with a clear abort, opens modified files in $EDITOR, and never creates a git commit automatically.
curatorDryrun() re-runs heuristics on an existing observations.jsonl using current config.curator.thresholds and prints rendered proposals without modifying canonical files.
curatorGc() keeps rows for the most recent runIds using default --keep 50 or explicit --keep <N>, rewrites only the rollup file, and is a no-op when fewer runs exist than the keep count.
The CLI reports clear helpful messages for missing runs, missing proposals.md, invalid run IDs, and absent --project by falling back to CWD project resolution.
suggestedCriteria: curatorStatus() prints a helpful message when no runs exist (no observations.jsonl found)
curatorStatus() prints a helpful message when observations.jsonl exists but proposals.md does not
curatorCommit() skips lines in proposals.md that are not checkbox items without error
curatorCommit() with an invalid runId prints a clear error message
curatorGc() is a no-op when there are fewer runs than --keep
Each curator subcommand properly handles missing --project by using CWD resolution

userStories.title: Document curator usage and architecture
userStories.description:
**Goal** — Add the documentation PR from the design spec so curator v0 is discoverable and operable after implementation.

**Motivation** — The source spec includes a documentation PR as part of v0 readiness. Without docs, users will not know that `review.audit.enabled` improves curator quality, where outputs are written after the folder split, how to accept proposals safely, or how to tune thresholds during dogfood.

**Approach** — Add `docs/guides/curator.md` covering what the curator reads, what it writes, how to enable/disable it, how to inspect proposals, how to use `nax curator commit`, and how threshold tuning works. Update `docs/architecture/subsystems.md` with a concise curator subsystem entry that explains projection-layer unification and explicitly says no LLM/auto-apply. Update README feature coverage. Update `docs/findings/2026-04-30-context-curator-design.md` status to point at the v0 spec/implementation.

**Scope** — In: documentation only. Out: code behavior changes and threshold calibration defaults.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/guides/curator.md explains curator inputs, outputs, configuration, review-audit dependency, proposal review flow, CLI commands, and threshold tuning
docs/architecture/subsystems.md includes a curator subsystem entry describing the built-in post-run plugin and projection-layer observations model
README mentions the curator as a deterministic post-run context maintenance feature
docs/findings/2026-04-30-context-curator-design.md status is updated to reference the implemented v0 spec/feature
Documentation states that curator never uses an LLM and never auto-applies canonical source changes without checked proposals plus `nax curator commit`

routing.reasoning: documentation-only story from source spec PR 7

suggestedCriteria: The guide includes the default output paths for both project artifacts and global rollup
The guide documents config.curator.enabled=false and config.disabledPlugins=["nax-curator"] as distinct disable options
The guide warns that rollup rows may contain project paths or story/context content and should be shared intentionally

analysis:
## Code-Backed Architecture Notes

The Context Curator remains a deterministic post-run projection plugin: no LLM calls, no automatic canonical-source edits, and no runtime event-log/blob-store unification. It reads existing artifacts, emits per-run observations, renders human-reviewable proposals, and appends observations to the configured rollup.

## Confirmed Current Code Shape

- src/runtime/paths.ts already implements the folder-split path layer: projectOutputDir(projectKey, config.outputDir) defaults to ~/.nax/<projectKey>, and curatorRollupPath(globalDir, config.curator?.rollupPath) defaults to ~/.nax/global/curator/rollup.jsonl.
- src/runtime/index.ts exposes runtime.outputDir, runtime.globalDir, runtime.projectKey, and runtime.curatorRollupPath, but src/plugins/extensions.ts / src/execution/lifecycle/run-cleanup.ts do not currently pass those fields into PostRunContext.
- src/plugins/loader.ts currently loads global/project/config plugins only. There is no built-in plugin registration path, so curator registration needs a small loader/registry addition.
- src/config/schemas-infra.ts currently has only curator.rollupPath; enabled and thresholds are not yet in schema or CuratorConfig.
- src/context/engine/manifest-store.ts stores manifests under project input .nax/features/<feature>/stories/<storyId>/context-manifest-<stage>.json; these are not under outputDir.
- src/metrics/tracker.ts saves generated metrics.json under runtime.outputDir.
- src/review/review-audit.ts writes review audit JSON under outputDir/review-audit/<feature>/ when runtime outputDir is available.
- The active run JSONL path is known to runner as logFilePath, but it is not currently included in post-run context.
- src/context/engine/pull-tools.ts and src/pipeline/stages/acceptance.ts do not yet emit the two curator-specific logger lines.
- ReviewDecisionEvent is optional. The code has direct recordDecision() call sites in semantic.ts, adversarial.ts, and semantic-debate.ts, but curator v0 can read review-audit JSON without changing that path.

## PRD Corrections Applied

- US-003 is included for implementation per product decision, while curator collection remains independent of the new event channel.
- US-004 now includes post-run context wiring, built-in plugin registration, outputDir/globalDir/rollup path usage, and grouped acceptance coverage for the observation families from the spec.
- US-005 now uses target.action consistently, clarifies that curatorRollupPath is a file path, and has grouped ACs for heuristics/render/rollup integration.
- US-006 now resolves generated artifacts through config plus runtime path helpers and has grouped ACs for status/commit/dryrun/gc behavior.
- US-007 was added for the documentation PR that was present in the spec but missing as an executable story.

## Deferred / Out Of Scope

- Dogfood and calibrated threshold defaults remain post-implementation follow-up work, not this PRD implementation scope.
- Domain inference for rule files remains deferred; v0 stages rules suggestions in .nax/rules/curator-suggestions.md.
- Event-log/blob-store unification remains explicitly out of scope.
